name: SQL Injection
description: An adversary uses SQL injection techniques to exploit the application's use of SQL to query a database.

name: XSS via HTTP Request Headers
description: An adversary crafts an HTTP request with a malicious header value in order to inject script code into the response.

name: Using Malicious Files
description: An adversary provides a file that appears legitimate but contains malicious code that executes when opened.

name: Buffer Overflow via Environment Variables
description: An adversary manipulates the length or content of environment variables to trigger a buffer overflow.

name: Excavation
description: An adversary engages in probing and exploration activity to determine and map the network perimeter.

name: Browser Fingerprinting
description: An adversary passively obtains information about a user's browser and system to track or identify them.

name: Phishing
description: An adversary masquerades as a legitimate entity in electronic communication to deceive victims into providing sensitive information.

name: Session Fixation
description: The adversary takes advantage of the application's predictable session identifier management to gain access to authenticated sessions.

name: Cross-Site Request Forgery
description: An adversary forges HTTP requests from a victim's browser to perform unauthorized actions.

name: Privilege Abuse
description: An adversary is able to elevate their permissions and access protected data or functionality.

name: Local Execution of Code
description: An adversary exploits a vulnerability in an application to execute arbitrary code on the local system.

name: Spear Phishing via Malicious File Downloads
description: An adversary sends targeted phishing messages containing links or attachments designed to deliver malware.

name: Adversary in the Middle (AiTM)
description: An adversary intercepts and potentially alters communications between two parties.

name: Reusing Session IDs (aka Session Replay)
description: An adversary steals a session identifier and reuses it to hijack an authenticated session.

name: Filter Failure through Buffer Overflow
description: An adversary exploits a buffer overflow to bypass input filters and inject malicious content.

name: Accessing/Intercepting/Modifying HTTP Cookies
description: An adversary intercepts HTTP cookies to hijack sessions or extract sensitive data.

name: Catching Exception Throw/Signal from Privileged Block
description: An adversary uses exception handling mechanisms to intercept signals or exceptions from privileged operations.

name: Use of Known Domain Credentials
description: An adversary uses compromised domain credentials obtained previously to authenticate to additional systems.

name: File Manipulation
description: An adversary manipulates files on the target system to inject malicious content or delete critical data.

name: PHP Remote File Inclusion
description: An adversary exploits improper handling of file inclusion to execute remote PHP code.
